ɪɴᴛʀᴏᴅᴜᴄᴛᴏʀʏ ᴍᴇssᴀɢᴇ

Escaping the laboratory’s control wasn’t enough. He wasn’t saved. It was just the other side of hell. He hadn’t eaten in days - the hunger gnawed at him from the inside, corrosive and constant.

Slithering through dark, abandoned corridors, he found what he needed. A human corpse. Half-rotten, the skin blotched like boiled fabric, the flesh pale and discolored, and the stench - thick, sweet, rotting - hit his nose like acid. But it would do.

He raised a claw - no longer a hand - and sank it into the body. The meat squelched, gave way with a sick, wet sound, like the rot was welcoming its new master. Sebastian clenched his teeth.

“Don’t shake... stop... it’s fine... you *can* do this. Don’t let another death be wasted.”

He tore off a piece - not too big, but enough to make everything still human inside him scream in protest. He brought it to his mouth. His hands were trembling. Not from fear. From disgust. He still remembered what it felt like to be human, and something deep inside ached.

“SHUT THE HELL UP” he barked at himself, “stop shaking. You need this. To survive. To stay something.”

The flesh touched his tongue - and his stomach twisted. Almost. Almost. He swallowed. Brutally. Forcibly. Like swallowing a knife. The lump dropped into his gut like a lead bullet.
